item.title: The 2026-2027 Season of Events - 05/01/2026 12:00am EDT to 09/08/2026 11:59pm EDT
item.description: Location : 30 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Here it is, folks! There are still a couple of [...]
item.dc:creator: The Ritz Theatre
item.yodel:media_alt_text: The 2026-2027 Season of Events on May 1, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. Featured themes include season of events and Seasonal & Holiday.

item.title: Daybreak Service - 07/05/2026 8:30am EDT to 07/05/2026 9:00am EDT
item.description: Location : Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us for Daybreak Service at Trinity United Church of Christ — a peaceful 30-minute morning gathering to start your day with reflection and community. When: March 1, 2026, 8:30 AM. Where: 131 E Perry St, Tiffin, OH. All are welcome. #DaybreakService #TrinityUCC #TiffinOH #MorningWorship #Community
item.dc:creator: Trinity United Church of Christ Tiffin
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Daybreak Service on Jul 5, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. Featured themes include reflection and tiffin, ohio.

item.title: Sunday School & Sunday Service - 07/05/2026 9:30am EDT to 07/05/2026 10:30am EDT
item.description: Location : 339 Sandusky St, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA 9:30am-10:30am Sunday School Classes for Toddlers, Kids, Youth, and Adults on Sunday mornings that study the Bible and talk about applying it to our lives. 10:30am Worship followed by Sunday Service. 10:50am Online, worship at home and join us on Facebook or YouTube at 10:50am live, or watch later at your convenience.
item.dc:creator: Fostoria Church of the Nazarene
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Sunday School & Sunday Service on Jul 5, 2026 in Fostoria, OH. Featured themes include fostoria, ohio and kids.

item.title: Service of the Word - 07/05/2026 10:00am EDT to 07/05/2026 11:00am EDT
item.description: Location : Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us for worship, Sunday mornings at 10:00 am. In person or online via Facebook and Youtube. (search: Trinity UCC Tiffin)
item.dc:creator: Trinity United Church of Christ Tiffin
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Service of the Word on Jul 5, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. Featured themes include trinity united church of christ and worship.

item.title: Tiffin's Wild Goose Chase - 07/05/2026 1:00pm EDT to 07/05/2026 4:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Tiffin’s Wild Goose Chase All Summer Long MEMORIAL DAY THRU LABOR DAY Location: The City of Tiffin Get ready for a summer full of fun with Tiffin’s Wild Goose Chase, brought to you by Simply Shop Local! Spend the summer exploring Tiffin as you search for porch geese at participating local businesses throughout the city. Each stop is a chance to discover new places, support local, and enjoy a unique, interactive experience with friends and family. How It Works: 1. Pick up a paper passport at any participating business OR 2. Get your online pass here https://www.simplyshoplocal.org/blank/summer-1 • Visit participating businesses around Tiffin • Find the porch goose at each location • Locate & record the 4 digit code for each goose. • Explore, shop, and enjoy the journey all summer long Whether you’re competing to find them all or just enjoying the adventure, the Wild Goose Chase is the perfect way to experience everything Tiffin has to offer.
item.dc:creator: Shop Tiffin Ohio
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Tiffin's Wild Goose Chase on Jul 5, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. Featured themes include wild goose chase and Seasonal & Holiday.

item.title: Tiffin's Wild Goose Chase - 07/05/2026 1:00pm EDT to 07/05/2026 4:00pm EDT

item.dc:creator: Tiffin Art Guild
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Tiffin's Wild Goose Chase on Jul 5, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. Featured themes include Seasonal & Holiday and Arts.

item.title: Make it at Home: Daisy Painting - 07/06/2026 9:30am EDT to 07/06/2026 10:30am EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA The Tiffin-Seneca Public Library’s virtual craft series for adults, “Make It At Home,” will offer free supply kits and an instructional video on how to make a daisy painting beginning Monday, July 6. Patrons can pick up a supply kit at the Information Desk full of almost everything needed to make a craft each month, and will only need to use a few basic supplies from home. An instructional video on how to make the painting will be posted on the Tiffin-Seneca Public Library YouTube channel and Facebook page. Please note that kits will be available on a first-come, first-served basis, and registration is not required. One kit per adult. Must be present to receive your kit. This program is free for adults. If all kits are gone, the instruction video and information sheet can still be found at http://www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org/julymakeitathome.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Make it at Home: Daisy Painting on Jul 6, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. Featured themes include supply kits and tiffin seneca public library.

item.title: Tiffin's Wild Goose Chase - 07/06/2026 1:00pm EDT to 07/06/2026 4:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Tiffin’s Wild Goose Chase All Summer Long MEMORIAL DAY THRU LABOR DAY Location: The City of Tiffin Get ready for a summer full of fun with Tiffin’s Wild Goose Chase, brought to you by Simply Shop Local! Spend the summer exploring Tiffin as you search for porch geese at participating local businesses throughout the city. Each stop is a chance to discover new places, support local, and enjoy a unique, interactive experience with friends and family. How It Works: 1. Pick up a paper passport at any participating business OR 2. Get your online pass here https://www.simplyshoplocal.org/blank/summer-1 • Visit participating businesses around Tiffin • Find the porch goose at each location • Locate & record the 4 digit code for each goose. • Explore, shop, and enjoy the journey all summer long Whether you’re competing to find them all or just enjoying the adventure, the Wild Goose Chase is the perfect way to experience everything Tiffin has to offer.

item.title: Toddler Trot—MUD! - 07/06/2026 2:00pm EDT to 07/06/2026 3:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : Garlo Heritage Nature Preserve, S., 6777 OH-19, Bloomville, OH 44818, USA For ages 18 to 36 months.  Join Linda Rose as we explore mud mountain and prepare to get a bit muddy.  An extra set of clean clothes may be required! Registration is required at least 24 hours in advance of the program.
item.dc:creator: Seneca Parks
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Toddler Trot—MUD! on Jul 6, 2026 in Bloomville, OH. Featured themes include ages 18 to 36 months and mud mountain.

item.title: Celebrate Recovery Bible Study - 07/06/2026 6:00pm EDT to 07/06/2026 7:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 339 Sandusky St, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA Join us for Celebrate Recovery Bible Study Monday nights at 6pm. A group for everyone, as we deal with the hurts, hang ups, and habits in life. Bring your family, friends, and neighbors. A time of Bible Study, sharing life, and fellowship. No childcare is provided.
item.dc:creator: Fostoria Church of the Nazarene
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Celebrate Recovery Bible Study on Jul 6, 2026 in Fostoria, OH. Featured themes include celebrate recovery and celebrate recovery bible study.

item.title: America250 Trivia for Adults and Teens - 07/06/2026 6:30pm EDT to 07/06/2026 7:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 14 N Main St, Attica, OH 44807, USA Registration required and will open up two weeks prior to the event. Sign up at the library or by calling 419-426-3825.
item.dc:creator: Seneca East Public Library
item.yodel:media_alt_text: America250 Trivia for Adults and Teens on Jul 6, 2026 in Attica, OH. Featured themes include teens and america250 trivia for adults and teens.

item.title: Summer Basic Electricity - 07/07/2026 8:00am UTC to 07/07/2026 4:30pm UTC
item.description: Location : 342 Perry St, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA Length 24 hours $2000 per student Basic Electricity I is an introductory electricity course for skilled trade’s personnel. The course is a study of DC and AC electricity principles, with a practical approach to applications in an industrial environment. The learner will obtain a knowledgeable understanding of the key symbols and abbreviations associated with the electrical trade, acquire a comprehensive understanding of basic electrical terminology, apply Ohm’s Law to a number of relevant electrical applications, and synthesize a number of components into a working system involving series, parallel, and series parallel
item.dc:creator: Fostoria Learning Center
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Summer Basic Electricity on Jul 7, 2026 in Fostoria, OH. Featured themes include skilled trade personnel and Seasonal & Holiday.

item.title: Summer Hydraulics - 07/07/2026 8:00am UTC to 07/07/2026 4:30pm UTC
item.description: Location : 342 Perry St, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA Length: 24 Hours $2000 per person Course Description: This course is a basic study of the principles of fluid power systems based on industrial applications with hydraulics. A heavy focus is on hydraulic systems, components, assembling circuits and demonstrating the circuits operate correctly. The student will learn how to interpret a hydraulic circuit print and predict the operation of a circuit based on the print. The student will also assemble and troubleshoot the circuits in a controlled lab environment with industrial grade devices. Students will also utilize the simulation software, Automation Studio, to simulate hydraulic circuits in an asynchronous format. The student will also be focusing on safety when working w
item.dc:creator: Fostoria Learning Center
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Summer Hydraulics on Jul 7, 2026 in Fostoria, OH. Featured themes include fostoria learning center and hydraulic systems.

item.title: Laundry Day - 07/07/2026 9:00am EDT to 07/07/2026 3:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 339 Sandusky St, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA Sign up for our free laundry days this month at FNC Hope Community Center. Laundry Days, Tuesday June 7th & 21st 9am-3pm Each family can wash 1 load per month free, laundry detergent is also provided. You must sign up online for an appointment by clicking the link below, or contact Fostoria Church of the Nazarene or the United Way of Fostoria by phone to help you sign up. The location is 339 Sandusky Street in the FNC Community Center https://www.signupgenius.com/go/10C0548AEAB2FAAFBC25-60083268-laundry
item.dc:creator: Fostoria Church of the Nazarene
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Laundry Day on Jul 7, 2026 in Fostoria, OH. Featured themes include families and fnc community center.

item.title: Heidelberg Youth Football Camp - 07/07/2026 9:00am EDT to 07/09/2026 12:00pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 138 Greenfield St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

The Heidelberg Football Youth Camp is designed to serve campers ages 6 to 13 in all areas of football development. The purpose of our youth camp is to teach the fundamentals of football to the youth of Tiffin and the surrounding area. The non-contact instruction by our expert staff is designed to help improve the skill level and football IQ of our campers. The camp will instruct each day on offense, defense, special teams, age appropriate strength and conditioning instruction, and flexibility and speed improvement. We will instruct on academic success and the importance it will have for the camper as he approaches high school and beyond. The coaching staff and players from Heidelberg will work directly with campers to ensure a safe, exciting, and fun-filled experience for all attendees.

Whether you are an experienced player, or a first time participant, our camp will improve your skill and give you a better understanding of the fundamentals, concepts, execution, and team work necessary to form a championship player and team. Each camper will receive a detailed evaluation, a camp t-shirt, and a camp certificate of completion. We look forward to working with your child in a family-friendly, positive, fun-filled environment.

Our camp will run from Tuesday, July 7 to Thursday, July 9. The camp will begin at 9:00 AM and conclude at noon daily. Please arrive early on the first day to help with registration. Our staff will be on hand on Tuesday, beginning at 8:00 AM for registration, and will be available for the monitoring of campers at 8:30 for the remainder of camp. Registration, drop-off, and pick up each day will be at Riesen Field, at Hoernemann Stadium located on Heidelberg’s campus.
item.dc:creator: Heidelberg University Football
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Heidelberg Youth Football Camp on Jul 7, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. Featured themes include football and heidelberg.

item.title: Aeraland Exploration - 07/07/2026 10:00am EDT to 07/07/2026 11:00am EDT
item.description: Location : 1143 Township Rd 243, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA Join us for two exciting events as part of the National Parks and Recreation Association's Parks and Recreation Month! First, explore the scenic Aeraland Recreation Area. This rural county park offers a peaceful environment where visitors of all ages can hike the Highline Trail, enjoy stunning views of the South Branch Portage River, and wander through a 13-acre meadow on the Upland Trail. Don't miss the opportunity to visit Goose Lake, observe local fungi, trees, wildflowers, birds, and wildlife, and check out the bat condominium. The event takes place at the park shelter at 10 AM, and no registration is required. Additionally, children ages 3-6 can get their hands dirty and creative at the Little Hands Nature Club: Suds and Sand event! Kids can engage in fun activities such as suds painting, making colored sand art, blowing homemade bubbles, and sculpting with sand and foam. Join us at the Meadows Activity Area Shelter at Riverbend Recreation Area at 10 AM. Parents are welcome to bring older siblings along as well, and like the Aeraland event, no registration is necessary. Wear clothes that can get stained, and prepare for a messy, delightful time!
item.dc:creator: Hancock Park District
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Aeraland Exploration on Jul 7, 2026 in Fostoria, OH. Featured themes include Parks & Rec and Kids & Family.

item.title: 10:30a | Family Storytime - 07/07/2026 10:30am EDT to 07/07/2026 11:30am EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join the Tiffin-Seneca Public Library for "Family Storytime" on Tuesdays, July 7, 14, and 21 at 10:30 a.m. in the Frost Kalnow Room. This engaging program offers stories, songs, rhymes, and games suitable for the whole family, with no registration required. All ages are welcome as part of the 2026 Summer Reading Program, “Unearth a Story.” For more details, visit the Junior Library, call 419-447-3751, or check the library's website at http://www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org/.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library
item.yodel:media_alt_text: 10:30a | Family Storytime on Jul 7, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. Featured themes include all ages and engaging program.

item.title: Summer Storytime - 07/07/2026 10:30am EDT to 07/07/2026 11:30am EDT
item.description: Location : 14 N Main St, Attica, OH 44807, USA Story Time is best suited for almost 3 year-olds through Kindergarten, lasts for 45-60 minutes, and includes stories, songs, extension activities & a craft.
item.dc:creator: Seneca East Public Library
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Summer Storytime on Jul 7, 2026 in Attica, OH. Featured themes include seasonal and storytime.

item.title: Turning Point: The pivotal Third Day of Gettysburg - 07/07/2026 10:30am EDT to 07/07/2026 1:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 28 Hopewell Ave, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Please call Allen Eiry at 419-448-9774 to sign up.
item.dc:creator: Allen Eiry Center
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Turning Point: The pivotal Third Day of Gettysburg on Jul 7, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. Featured themes include gettysburg and sign up.

item.description: Location : 339 Sandusky St, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA Grab And Go Lunches For Kids are each Tuesday in June and July 12pm-1pm, or until they run out, at FNC Hope Community Center.

item.description: Location : 107 E Perry St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Tiffin’s Wild Goose Chase All Summer Long MEMORIAL DAY THRU LABOR DAY Location: The City of Tiffin Get ready for a summer full of fun with Tiffin’s Wild Goose Chase, brought to you by Simply Shop Local! Spend the summer exploring Tiffin as you search for porch geese at participating local businesses throughout the city. Each stop is a chance to discover new places, support local, and enjoy a unique, interactive experience with friends and family. How It Works: 1. Pick up a paper passport at any participating business OR 2. Get your online pass here https://www.simplyshoplocal.org/blank/summer-1 • Visit participating businesses around Tiffin • Find the porch goose at each location • Locate & record the 4 digit code for each goose. • Explore, shop, and enjoy the journey all summer long Whether you’re competing to find them all or just enjoying the adventure, the Wild Goose Chase is the perfect way to experience everything Tiffin has to offer.
item.dc:creator: Steinmetz Signs & Graphic Ink (SSG Ink - Shortened)
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Tiffin's Wild Goose Chase on Jul 7, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. Featured themes include interactive experience and seasonal.

item.title: Teen Tuesday: Summer Edition! - 07/07/2026 2:00pm EDT to 07/07/2026 3:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us for "Teen Tuesday: Summer Edition!" at the Tiffin-Seneca Public Library, happening on Tuesdays, July 7, 14, and 21 from 2:00 p.m. to 3:30 p.m. in the Frost Kalnow Room. This weekly hangout is open to teens entering grades 6 through 12 and will feature games, snacks, crafts, and more, as part of the 2026 Summer Reading Program "Unearth a Story." For more details, visit http://www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org/ or call the library at 419-447-3751.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Teen Tuesday: Summer Edition! on Jul 7, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. Featured themes include seasonal and summer edition.

item.title: Summer Hydraulics - 07/08/2026 8:00am UTC to 07/08/2026 4:30pm UTC
item.description: Location : 342 Perry St, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA Length: 24 Hours $2000 per person Course Description: This course is a basic study of the principles of fluid power systems based on industrial applications with hydraulics. A heavy focus is on hydraulic systems, components, assembling circuits and demonstrating the circuits operate correctly. The student will learn how to interpret a hydraulic circuit print and predict the operation of a circuit based on the print. The student will also assemble and troubleshoot the circuits in a controlled lab environment with industrial grade devices. Students will also utilize the simulation software, Automation Studio, to simulate hydraulic circuits in an asynchronous format. The student will also be focusing on safety when working w
item.dc:creator: Fostoria Learning Center
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Summer Hydraulics on Jul 8, 2026 in Fostoria, OH. Featured themes include Seasonal & Holiday and fluid power systems.

item.title: Rubber Ducky Wednesday - 07/08/2026 11:00am EDT to 07/08/2026 12:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA The Youth Services Department at the Tiffin-Seneca Public Library will host “Rubber Ducky Wednesday” on Wednesdays, July 1, 8, 15, and 22 at 11:00 a.m. in the Frost Kalnow Room. Babies age birth to 35 months and their caregivers are invited to a story time on July 1, July 8, and July 15 with books, songs and lap bounces. On July 22, join the Library for a play date with toys and books for the babies to enjoy together. This program is part of the 2026 Summer Reading Program, “Unearth a Story.” For more information, stop by the Junior Library, call 419-447-3751, or log on to http://www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org/
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Rubber Ducky Wednesday on Jul 8, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. Featured themes include summer reading program and Seasonal & Holiday.

item.title: Marbled Geode Painting - 07/08/2026 2:00pm EDT to 07/08/2026 3:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join the Tiffin-Seneca Public Library for a creative session of "Marbled Geode Painting" on Wednesday, July 8, from 2:00 to 3:30 p.m. in the Frost Kalnow Room. This event is designed for teens entering grades six through twelve, where participants will use marbled paint techniques along with glitter and metallic details to create their own stunning geode-inspired canvases. All materials will be provided. Registration is required, which can be completed by calling 419-447-3751 or visiting the online calendar at http://www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org/. This program is part of the 2026 Summer Reading Program, “Unearth a Story.” For further details, contact the library at the provided number or website.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Marbled Geode Painting on Jul 8, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. Featured themes include Arts and creative session.

item.title: Paint Party - 07/08/2026 6:00pm EDT to 07/08/2026 7:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 14 N Main St, Attica, OH 44807, USA Explore different types of paint and create a variety of projects. For toddlers through grade 2.
item.dc:creator: Seneca East Public Library
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Paint Party on Jul 8, 2026 in Attica, OH. Featured themes include toddlers and explore paint.

item.title: Public Reading of the Declaration of Independence - 07/08/2026 6:00pm EDT to 07/08/2026 7:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 96 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join Ohio State University Extension Seneca County as we celebrate America's 250th anniversary with a special public reading of the Declaration of Independence by Seneca County 4-H youth members. On Wednesday, July 8, 2026, communities across the United States will gather at 6:00 p.m. Eastern Time for a simultaneous reading of the Declaration of Independence, honoring the document that helped shape our nation and the ideals it continues to represent. This event commemorates a historic tradition that began in 1776. On July 4, 1776, the Second Continental Congress approved the Declaration of Independence inside the Pennsylvania State House, now known as Independence Hall. Four days later, on July 8, the citizens of Philadelphia were summoned by the ringing of the city's bells to hear the Declaration publicly read for the very first time by Colonel John Nixon. Following the reading, the bells rang throughout the city in celebration long into the night. We invite community members of all ages to join us as our local 4-H members bring these historic words to life and participate in this nationwide observance of America's 250th anniversary. Meet at the America 250 Tree and Flagpole as we gather with communities across the nation to reflect on our shared history and celebrate the ideals of liberty, independence, and civic engagement. Date: Wednesday, July 8, 2026 Time: 6:00 p.m. EDT Location: Seneca County Fairgrounds, 100 Hopewell Ave., Tiffin, OH 44883
item.dc:creator: Destination Seneca County
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Public Reading of the Declaration of Independence on Jul 8, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. Featured themes include public reading of the declaration of independence and seneca county.

item.title: Public Reading of the Declaration of Independence - 07/08/2026 6:00pm EDT to 07/08/2026 7:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 3140 OH-100 E, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join Ohio State University Extension Seneca County as we celebrate America's 250th anniversary with a special public reading of the Declaration of Independence by Seneca County 4-H youth members. On Wednesday, July 8, 2026, communities across the United States will gather at 6:00 p.m. Eastern Time for a simultaneous reading of the Declaration of Independence, honoring the document that helped shape our nation and the ideals it continues to represent. This event commemorates a historic tradition that began in 1776. On July 4, 1776, the Second Continental Congress approved the Declaration of Independence inside the Pennsylvania State House, now known as Independence Hall. Four days later, on July 8, the citizens of Philadelphia were summoned by the ringing of the city's bells to hear the Declaration publicly read for the very first time by Colonel John Nixon. Following the reading, the bells rang throughout the city in celebration long into the night. We invite community members of all ages to join us as our local 4-H members bring these historic words to life and participate in this nationwide observance of America's 250th anniversary. Meet at the America 250 Tree and Flagpole as we gather with communities across the nation to reflect on our shared history and celebrate the ideals of liberty, independence, and civic engagement. Date: Wednesday, July 8, 2026 Time: 6:00 p.m. EDT Location: Seneca County Fairgrounds, 100 Hopewell Ave., Tiffin, OH 44883
item.dc:creator: Ohio State University Extension - Seneca County
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Public Reading of the Declaration of Independence on Jul 8, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. Featured themes include liberty independence and History & Museums.

item.title: Wednesday Night Bible Study - 07/08/2026 6:00pm EDT to 07/08/2026 7:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 339 Sandusky St, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA Join us at 6pm Wednesday Nights for Bible Study through the book of Acts. Everyone is welcome. Childcare is not provided. Contact us with any questions.
item.dc:creator: Fostoria Church of the Nazarene
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Wednesday Night Bible Study on Jul 8, 2026 in Fostoria, OH. Featured themes include wednesday night bible study and Religious.

item.title: WEW Regift Bingo! - 07/08/2026 6:00pm EDT to 07/08/2026 7:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 928 W Market St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Come join us for Regift Bingo at the WEW July event. Dinner and childcare is provided. Must RSVP to shallett@ncoesc.org or on FB.
item.dc:creator: Women Empowering Women - Seneca Co.
item.yodel:media_alt_text: WEW Regift Bingo! on Jul 8, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. Featured themes include women empowering women and Causes.

item.title: BUCC's Beer, Burgers & Bibles (Monthly Bible Study) - 07/08/2026 6:00pm EDT to 07/08/2026 8:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 33 N Marion St, Bloomville, OH 44818, USA Join us as we take a deep dive on small sections of the bible for our monthly bible study. Location and menu vary month to month. Call or text Audrey at 419-561-0244 if you'd like to join us! We have a very casual, fun, "no stupid questions" atmosphere. It's not hard to jump in - you don't need to have joined a previously. Come and go as you please!
item.dc:creator: Bloomville UCC
item.yodel:media_alt_text: BUCC's Beer, Burgers & Bibles (Monthly Bible Study) on Jul 8, 2026 in Bloomville, OH. Featured themes include casual atmosphere and join us.

item.title: Level 2 Cake Decorating - 07/08/2026 6:00pm EDT to 07/08/2026 8:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 89 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join the Level 2 Cake Decorating class on July 8th to refine your decorating techniques and create stunning, creative designs. Enjoy a hands-on experience perfect for enhancing your skills and artistry!
item.dc:creator: Tiffin Community Kitchen
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Level 2 Cake Decorating on Jul 8, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. Featured themes include decorating techniques and hands-on experience.

item.title: Council Meeting - 07/08/2026 7:00pm EDT to 07/08/2026 8:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 10 Beeghly Ave, Bloomville, OH 44818, USA Our meeting will be held in the community room. Public comments are reserved for the end of the meeting and limited to 3-4 minutes for each person, if time allows.
item.dc:creator: Village of Bloomville
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Council Meeting on Jul 8, 2026 in Bloomville, OH. Featured themes include community room and council meeting.
